name: Genius Sports Limited

profile.description: Genius Sports Limited is a sports data and technology company that provides data management, video streaming, and integrity services to sports leagues, betting operators, and media companies worldwide. The company's GeniusIQ AI platform captures and processes next-generation sports data using machine learning and generative AI. Key partnerships include the NFL, Premier League, NCAA, and major global sports organizations. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in London, with operations across 20+ countries.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Genius Sports has transformed from a sports data collection company into a full AI-powered sports technology platform, launching GeniusIQ — its next-generation machine learning and generative AI platform — and completing the transformative Legend acquisition in 2026 that raised revenue guidance from $810M to approximately $1B [1]. The company secured exclusive data partnerships with the NFL, Premier League, NCAA, and other major leagues, creating durable competitive moats. Revenue has grown from $263M in 2021 to $669M in 2025 (26% CAGR), representing genuine business model evolution. AI Deployment (2/2): GeniusIQ leverages powerful machine learning and generative AI to deliver game analysis and immersive fan experiences, generating measurable revenue across data, streaming, and integrity services [2]. The appointment of former Google Technical Director Mark Kropf as Group CTO specifically to accelerate AI capabilities signals deep commitment to AI-driven product development [2]. Market Share (1/2): Genius Sports holds exclusive official data partnerships with major sports leagues including the NFL and Premier League, creating structural advantages in the sports data market [1]. However, the company competes with Sportradar and other technology providers, and market leadership is segment-specific rather than industry-wide. Product Pipeline (1/2): The Legend acquisition closed in 2026 with specific financial targets of $990M-$1.01B revenue and 28% adjusted EBITDA margin, providing clear near-term visibility [1]. 2028 financial targets presented at Investor Day offer longer-term pipeline clarity. R&D Intensity (1/1): Hiring Google's former AI-focused CTO and making technology development the central strategic pillar places Genius Sports above peers in sports technology R&D investment intensity [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Genius Sports positions itself as powering the ecosystem of global sport through data and technology, aiming to create the foundational technology infrastructure for sports worldwide [2]. The purpose is clear and identifies who the company serves and why, but it is not deeply distinctive — 'technology infrastructure for sport' is commercially oriented rather than transformative. It doesn't articulate a vision of a better future beyond better sports data. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable foundations, community investment programs, or philanthropy beyond core business operations was identified. The sports data business supports league integrity and anti-corruption efforts, but these are commercial services rather than charitable impact. Employee Retention (0/2): No specific employee retention, tenure, or turnover data is publicly available. The company has approximately 1,800 employees across 20+ countries, but cultural quality evidence is limited. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO Mark Locke, Chairman Kenneth Kay, and the Legend Founder collectively purchased approximately 1.4 million shares in February 2026, demonstrating significant insider conviction during a period of stock weakness [3]. Locke holds over 20 million shares personally, representing meaningful alignment with shareholders.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 revenue of $188M grew 31% YoY with adjusted EBITDA of $24.1M, and management raised full-year guidance to $990M-$1.01B revenue (approximately 48% growth including Legend) with 28% EBITDA margin target [1]. D/E of 4% reflects a nearly debt-free balance sheet with $197.4M cash against $30.6M total debt. Revenue growth is exceptional — 31% YoY and 26.3% 5-year CAGR — scoring maximum on growth metrics. Forward P/E of 29.31 is above the 20 target but below the 60 floor, scoring partially. The key weakness is persistent unprofitability: profit margin of -22.27% and ROE of -22.60% both score zero. The improving loss trajectory (EPS from -$3.79 in 2021 to -$0.44 in 2025) and positive operating cash flow of $50.8M suggest a path to profitability, but current GAAP losses remain substantial [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): Genius Sports occupies a complex position — it is both an AI company and exposed to AI disruption. The company's core moat comes from exclusive official data partnerships with the NFL, Premier League, NCAA, and other leagues that AI competitors cannot replicate [1]. These contractual relationships and the physical data collection infrastructure (cameras, sensors, on-site personnel) create structural barriers. However, the AI-powered analytics, streaming, and integrity services that generate revenue could face competitive pressure from well-resourced technology companies (Google, Amazon, Microsoft) entering sports data with superior AI capabilities. Sportradar is a direct competitor investing heavily in AI. The sports betting ecosystem itself faces regulatory uncertainty that could constrain the total addressable market. AI Opportunity (1/1): GeniusIQ generates meaningful revenue through AI-powered products — sports data analytics, real-time streaming, betting integrity monitoring, and fan engagement tools all leverage machine learning and generative AI as core product capabilities [2]. The appointment of Google's former AI-focused CTO validates the company's AI revenue generation potential.

infinite_game.summary: Genius Sports is a growth-oriented technology company focused on market capture and financial targets — fundamentally finite objectives. The Legend acquisition, raised revenue guidance, and 2028 Investor Day targets reflect a company playing to win market share in sports data rather than playing to endure. CEO Mark Locke's strategy is commercially effective but oriented toward scale and profitability milestones rather than an enduring purpose beyond powering sports technology.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Genius Sports articulates a purpose of creating foundational technology infrastructure for global sport, which is commercially clear but falls short of Sinek's Just Cause criteria [2]. The purpose describes what the company builds (sports data infrastructure), not a vision of a future state that inspires sacrifice. CEO Mark Locke's public communications emphasize revenue growth, EBITDA margins, and acquisition synergies — the language of a company playing to win, not to advance a cause. The purpose would need to articulate how sport makes the world better and how Genius Sports uniquely advances that vision to qualify as a genuine Just Cause.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The ability to attract top talent from Google (CTO Mark Kropf) suggests a culture that appeals to high-caliber technologists, and the global team of approximately 1,800 employees across 20+ countries requires some degree of organizational trust [2]. The Legend acquisition integration will test team cohesion as two organizations merge. No specific employee satisfaction, retention, or cultural data is publicly available to distinguish the organizational culture.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Genius Sports competes directly with Sportradar and other sports data companies, but management communications frame the competitive landscape in terms of market position and exclusive partnerships rather than acknowledging rivals' strengths. The Legend acquisition was explicitly described as strengthening competitive positioning and raising financial targets — language oriented toward winning market share rather than learning from competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Genius Sports has evolved meaningfully from a sports data collection and integrity monitoring company into an AI-powered technology platform, pivoting its business model from labor-intensive data capture to AI-driven analytics, streaming, and fan engagement [1][2]. The GeniusIQ platform and Legend acquisition represent genuine business model evolution, though the changes are incremental expansions into adjacent capabilities rather than a transformative pivot that abandoned a core business.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The Legend acquisition is a bold move — it raises revenue guidance by nearly 25% and targets 28% EBITDA margin, but it also requires successful integration of two companies while Genius Sports is still unprofitable on a GAAP basis [1]. Hiring Google's former AI-focused CTO and investing heavily in GeniusIQ before achieving profitability shows willingness to prioritize long-term capability over near-term earnings. CEO Locke's and Chairman Kay's collective purchase of 1.4M shares at depressed prices demonstrates personal financial commitment [3].

investment_thesis: Genius Sports delivered Q1 2026 revenue of $188M (31% YoY growth) with adjusted EBITDA of $24.1M, then raised full-year 2026 guidance to $990M-$1.01B revenue and $270-$280M adjusted EBITDA (28% margin) following the Legend acquisition close [1]. The company's exclusive data partnerships with the NFL, Premier League, and NCAA create structural moats that competitors cannot replicate, while the GeniusIQ AI platform — now led by former Google CTO Mark Kropf — positions Genius Sports at the intersection of sports data and generative AI [2]. With a nearly debt-free balance sheet ($197M cash, $30.6M debt), positive operating cash flow of $50.8M, and insider purchases of 1.4M shares at depressed prices, the fundamental trajectory is improving rapidly toward sustainable profitability.
thesis_continued: The stock has declined 46% over the past 52 weeks despite strong operational performance, reflecting market skepticism about the path to GAAP profitability and the Legend integration execution risk [4]. At $1.46B market cap on a $1B revenue run-rate, the valuation appears undemanding, but persistent GAAP losses (-$158.8M TTM net income) and 11% YoY share dilution erode shareholder value. The gambling exposure carries regulatory risk — changes to sports betting legalization, advertising restrictions, or responsible gambling requirements could constrain the total addressable market. The Legend acquisition increases integration complexity and raises the execution bar. Forward P/E of 29.31 assumes a profitability inflection that has not yet occurred, and the 1.92 beta means any market downturn will amplify losses in this name.
key_catalysts: Successful Legend integration delivering on the raised guidance of $990M-$1.01B revenue with 28% adjusted EBITDA margin, proving the acquisition thesis
GeniusIQ AI platform generating measurable revenue gains from new product categories and expanded monetization of official league data partnerships
GAAP profitability inflection as the adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of 500bps translates into positive net income, validating the long-term business model
key_risks: Legend acquisition integration complexity could delay the targeted 28% adjusted EBITDA margin and $1B revenue, while 11% YoY share dilution signals ongoing shareholder value erosion
Regulatory changes to sports betting legalization, advertising restrictions, or responsible gambling requirements in key markets could constrain total addressable market growth

GAAP unprofitability persists with -$158.8M TTM net loss — the forward P/E of 29.31 assumes a profitability inflection that is not guaranteed, and any miss could further pressure the stock below $5.44
